title: Add emission smoothing calibration: compare add-one, Witten-Bell, and absolute-discounting on held-out D3 partition
body:
Emission tables were always compiled with add-one smoothing (`P(v|n) = (count+1)/(total+V)`) without empirical justification. This adds a calibration step that compares all three smoothing families on held-out bits/token and top-1 agreement, records the winner in `score_report.json`, and uses the chosen rule for compilation.

## New infrastructure (`score.rs`)

- **`EmissionSmoothingRule` enum** — `AddOne`, `WittenBell`, `AbsoluteDiscounting(f64)` with a `label()` method for deterministic report strings
- **Smoothing probability helpers** — `add_one_prob`, `witten_bell_emission_prob`, `absolute_discounting_emission_prob`; all back off to the add-one-smoothed root prior
- **`evaluate_smoothing_rule`** — evaluates one rule on the held-out set given precomputed region evidence; returns `(bits_per_token, top1_agreement)`
- **`calibrate_emission_smoothing`** — public API; builds evidence with the same semantics as `compile_emissions` (top-3 teacher-weighted counts over covered binary top-1 members), evaluates add-one + Witten-Bell + absolute-discounting at δ ∈ {0.1, 0.2, …, 1.0}, sorts candidates by bits/token, and returns `EmissionSmoothingCalibration`
- **`SmoothingCandidate` / `EmissionSmoothingCalibration`** — serializable structs embedded in `score_report.json` under `smoothing_calibration`; record all losers' numbers alongside the chosen rule and margin

## Schema changes

- `ScoreConfig` gains `emission_smoothing_rule: String` — set by the calibration result before `compile_emissions` runs
- `ScoreReport` bumped to **schema 3**; adds `smoothing_calibration: EmissionSmoothingCalibration`
- `ScoreReportConfig` exposes `emission_smoothing_rule` so the report is self-describing

## Pipeline (`command.rs`)

`score_command` runs `calibrate_emission_smoothing` immediately after evidence is available (before `compile_emissions`), injects the winning rule into `ScoreConfig`, and passes the calibration struct through to `build_score_report`.

## Determinism

Evidence computation is identical to `compile_emissions` (BTreeMap accumulations, content-addressed observation order); candidate sort is stable (bits/token ascending, then label). Two runs on identical inputs produce byte-identical calibration JSON.

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->

- Fixes #67

title: Gate C F-emissions ablation: drop ΔT offset from Rule-1 scorer
body:
This PR reruns the F-emissions ablation under the fixed scorer by comparing Gate C with transition-edge scores enabled vs zeroed, with all other inputs held constant. The measured delta shows no top-1 gain from ΔT, so scorer policy is updated to drop ΔT offset application.

- **Ablation result + decision (recorded)**
  - Added the 4-way Gate C comparison to docs (enabled/zeroed × no-EXCT/with-EXCT).
  - Recorded decision: **drop ΔT offset**; keep `E_f` only for predicted-cloud candidate generation.
  - Included a transition-edge calibration spot-check sample (top edges by corpus count) for quick eyeballing.

- **Scorer semantics update**
  - Updated Rule-1 scoring to remove folded transition-edge weight from per-token score accumulation.
  - `transition_offset` remains in witness schema for compatibility, but is now always zero by policy.

- **Fixture ablation workload**
  - Added an ignored release-only fixture test that emits both variants (enabled vs zeroed), computes Gate C for both, and prints the 4-way table and deltas.

- **Tests adjusted for new policy**
  - Updated hand-computed and overlap-focused score expectations that previously included `+ ΔT-offset`.

```rust
// score_runtime.rs (Rule 1 scoring path)
let transition_offset = ScoreQ::ZERO;

for (token, (residual, mut contributions)) in candidates {
    let base = self.root_score(token);
    let score = base.saturating_add(residual); // ΔT offset no longer applied
    // ...
}
```

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->

- Fixes #66

title: Add score_candidates_legacy, sibling double-counting test, Gate C legacy column, and canonical bits/token definition
body:
Gate C measured the Σ-over-cloud formula at 0.3% top-1 agreement vs 31.7% TLA3 baseline (issue #64). The chain-telescoped redesign and EXCT precedence are already in `score_candidates`, but `score_candidates_legacy` — referenced in module docs as the comparison baseline — was never implemented, and no test proved the stacking was actually fixed.

## `score_candidates_legacy` (`score_runtime.rs`)

Implements the original formula for Gate C side-by-side comparison only:
```
S(v) = B(v) + Σ_{n∈A} ΔE(n,v) + transition_offset + ΔX(X,v)
```
All active regions contribute ΔE (no chain restriction) — the double-counting path.

## Sibling double-counting test (`tests/score.rs`)

`legacy_formula_double_counts_sibling_regions_new_formula_does_not`: two sibling depth-1 regions, both active for the same context, both emitting ΔE=60 on token 10 against B(token 20)=100:

| Formula | S(token 10) | S(token 20) | Winner |
|---|---|---|---|
| Legacy Σ-cloud | 10+60+60 = **130** | 100 | token 10 ❌ |
| Chain-telescoped | 10+60 = **70** | 100 | token 20 ✓ |

## Gate C legacy column (`score.rs`, `command.rs`)

`GateCOutcome` gains `graph_legacy: GateCMetrics`; `evaluate_gate_c` runs the legacy scorer alongside the chain-telescoped and EXCT scorers. The CLI now emits a 4-row table: `graph (no EXCT)` / `graph (with EXCT)` / `graph (legacy Σ-cloud)` / `TLA3 store baseline`.

## Canonical bits/token definition (`BASELINE.md §4.1`, Chain 4 item 13)

Disambiguates the two figures in the baseline: Gate C scorer-accounting (11.88 TLA3 / 70.47 v1 graph) is the normative M.V.G. gating metric; Witten-Bell (6.54 P2) is for certificate continuity and is not the gate.

## Risk register update (`r4_graph_compiler_implementation_plan.md §10`)

Double-counting entry updated from CONFIRMED to RESOLVED, pointing at the implementation and the new test.

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->

- Fixes #65

title: Evaluate overlap-node alternatives: cloud-size normalization and margin weighting

milestone.title: Phase 4: Transitions and residual emission
milestone.description: Forward/reverse indexes, root priors, ScoreQ residuals, interaction residuals, bounded top-K. Gates A/C.

body:
Depends on: #64 must land first and show its numbers. Reserved candidate from the #64 redesign list that chain-telescoping did NOT implement: normalize emission sums by contributing-region count, or weight contributions by membership margin/support. If the chain-telescoped Gate C result still underperforms the baseline on uncovered contexts, implement margin-weighted chain selection (or normalization) as a measured variant and compare on the 4-way table. Acceptance: either a measured improvement adopted in the scorer, or a recorded negative result closing the candidate.


---

## Definition of Done
- The reserve candidates (cloud-size normalization, margin/support weighting) are evaluated as measured scorer variants against the chain-telescoped baseline — or closed untriggered because #64's numbers made them unnecessary.
- The outcome is a measured adoption or an attached negative result.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Evaluation happens only after #64's 4-way table exists; the trigger condition (underperformance on uncovered contexts) is explicitly checked first.
- [ ] Each variant has its own Gate C row (top-1, bits/token) vs the chain-telescoped row.
- [ ] Adoption requires a non-noise improvement on the D3 held-out set; otherwise the negative result table is attached and the candidate closed.

title: Repetition control in the graph scoring path

milestone.title: Phase 5: Allocation-free graph runtime
milestone.description: Fixed-capacity RuntimeState, step API, normative kernel, ResolutionStatus, witness replay, M.V.G. checkpoint. Gate B.

body:
Depends on: #64. The legacy runtime penalizes recently-emitted tokens via the recency Vec (now TokenState); the graph scorer has no equivalent, so greedy graph generation can loop. Add a bounded, integer-only repetition mechanism to the graph scorer (recency window penalty in ScoreQ, or coverage-aware suppression), measured by repetition rate on a fixed probe set. Acceptance: repetition rate reported in score_report.json; no quality regression on Gate C; allocation-free and integer-only.


---

## Definition of Done
- A bounded, integer-only repetition mechanism exists in the graph scorer (ScoreQ recency penalty or coverage-aware suppression).
- Repetition rate is measured and reported without Gate C quality regression.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Repetition rate on a fixed probe set is reported in score_report.json (before vs after).
- [ ] Gate C top-1 agreement and bits/token unchanged within noise.
- [ ] The mechanism is allocation-free and integer-only (census + source scan).

title: ResolutionStatus-driven behavior in the deployed path

milestone.title: Phase 5: Allocation-free graph runtime
milestone.description: Fixed-capacity RuntimeState, step API, normative kernel, ResolutionStatus, witness replay, M.V.G. checkpoint. Gate B.

body:
Depends on: #64 (statuses exist in the scorer), residualized EXCT. The scorer emits ExactContext/Graph/Novel statuses but the deployed adapter (src/r4g1.rs) has no behavior attached. Wire the D4 manifest policy: ExactContext → serve; Graph → serve; Novel → widen candidate set once, then abstain with explicit status (never silently guess); Contradictory → abstain. Acceptance: per-status behavior table in the manifest; server responses surface status; adversarial/OOD probe suite shows abstention instead of pathological output (the current reject guard becomes the last line, not the only one).


---

## Definition of Done
- The D4 manifest policy is wired into the deployed adapter: per-status behavior for ExactContext / Graph / Novel / Contradictory is declared and enforced.
- Server responses surface the status; adversarial/OOD inputs abstain rather than emit pathological text.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Manifest carries the per-status behavior table (serve / widen-once / consult EXCT / abstain).
- [ ] API responses include the ResolutionStatus field.
- [ ] A probe suite (OOD + adversarial) shows widen-then-abstain behavior with the status visible; the pathological-output reject guard fires only as the last line.
- [ ] Zero allocation and integer-only contract preserved in the new path (census test updated).

title: CI Gate C trend tracking with regression alarms

milestone.title: Phase 5: Allocation-free graph runtime
milestone.description: Fixed-capacity RuntimeState, step API, normative kernel, ResolutionStatus, witness replay, M.V.G. checkpoint. Gate B.

body:
Depends on: 4-way Gate C harness (part of #64 finish) and canonical bits/token. Record the 4-way Gate C table per main commit (small corpus slice for speed) as a CI artifact with a regression threshold (e.g. top-1 drops > 2 points or bits/token worsens > 0.1 vs the pinned previous record → fail or loud annotation). Acceptance: ci.yml uploads the trend JSON; two consecutive commits demonstrate the alarm path on a synthetic regression.


---

## Definition of Done
- CI records the 4-way Gate C table (small corpus slice) per main commit as an artifact, with a regression alarm on a pinned threshold.
- The alarm path is demonstrated end-to-end.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] ci.yml uploads the trend JSON per run with commit metadata.
- [ ] A synthetic regression (top-1 drop > 2 points or bits/token +0.1 vs pinned previous) demonstrably triggers the alarm in CI logs.
- [ ] The threshold and the trend artifact location are documented in the workflow header.

title: Canonical bits/token definition across harnesses

milestone.title: Phase 5: Allocation-free graph runtime
milestone.description: Fixed-capacity RuntimeState, step API, normative kernel, ResolutionStatus, witness replay, M.V.G. checkpoint. Gate B.

body:
Depends on: none. The repo currently reports bits/token in at least two accountings: the P2 certificate's 6.54 (WB metric) and the Gate C scorer's 11.88 — different metric families that will confuse the M.V.G. checkpoint. Pick one canonical definition (documented formula, nats→bits conversion, which distributions it covers), implement it in the Gate C harness and the certificate path, and annotate the historical values as their own families. Acceptance: one formula in GLOSSARY.md + all future reports cite it; BASELINE.md updated.


---

## Definition of Done
- One canonical bits/token definition (formula, nats→bits conversion, covered distributions) is documented in `docs/transformerless/GLOSSARY.md`.
- The Gate C harness and the certificate path both emit the canonical metric; historical values are annotated as their own families.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] The formula appears verbatim in GLOSSARY.md and is referenced by every subsequent report.
- [ ] score_report.json and the certificate schema carry the canonical field name.
- [ ] BASELINE.md marks 6.54 (P2 WB) and 11.88 (scorer) as their historical families with the mapping note.

title: HF-path (SmolLM2) full Gate C on the D3 distribution

milestone.title: Phase 5: Allocation-free graph runtime
milestone.description: Fixed-capacity RuntimeState, step API, normative kernel, ResolutionStatus, witness replay, M.V.G. checkpoint. Gate B.

body:
Depends on: #64, D3 observation run. The M.V.G. checkpoint's judge must run on the actual deployment model and declared distribution, not the legacy fixture corpus. Run cover + score + Gate C for the SmolLM2-135M compile on the D3 partitions (teacher-generated continuity + Simple Wiki natural), producing the checkpoint inputs for BASELINE.md §4 targets 1-2. Acceptance: a score_report.json per partition with the 4-way table; the §4 target rows filled with measured values (pass or fail stated plainly).


---

## Definition of Done
- Cover + score + Gate C run end-to-end for the SmolLM2-135M compile on both D3 partitions (teacher-generated continuity + Simple Wiki natural).
- The M.V.G. target rows 1–2 in `docs/transformerless/BASELINE.md` §4 are filled with measured values and plain pass/fail verdicts.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] One `score_report.json` per partition with the full 4-way table, posted in this thread.
- [ ] BASELINE.md §4 rows 1–2 updated with measured agreement and bits/token vs the HF-path TLA3 baseline.
- [ ] Any gap between fixture-corpus and D3-distribution numbers is analyzed (not just reported).

title: Richer teacher evidence: top-k > 3 observation records

milestone.title: Phase 4: Transitions and residual emission
milestone.description: Forward/reverse indexes, root priors, ScoreQ residuals, interaction residuals, bounded top-K. Gates A/C.

body:
Depends on: nothing (independent of formula; changes record format — coordinate with any in-flight record work). Observation records currently store top-3 teacher tokens + weights. Residual/emission quality may improve with deeper evidence (top-8 or full top-k over a threshold). Measure the artifact-byte cost vs Gate C gain of top-8 records on the fixture corpus (v4 record format, backward-compatible loader like v3). Acceptance: byte-delta table and Gate C delta; adopt only if the agreement gain justifies the bytes (record the threshold).


---

## Definition of Done
- A v4 observation record format with top-8 teacher tokens + weights exists (backward-compatible loader, like v3), and its byte cost vs Gate C gain is measured.
- An adopt/reject decision is recorded with a documented threshold.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Byte-delta per record and total corpus size delta reported.
- [ ] Gate C top-1 agreement and bits/token with top-8 evidence vs top-3, deterministic double-run.
- [ ] Adopt only if the agreement gain justifies the bytes per the recorded threshold; otherwise the table is attached as the rejection evidence.

title: Larger-teacher quality ceiling evaluation

milestone.title: Phase 2: Overlapping multiresolution regions
milestone.description: Cover induction, calibrated radii, bounded multi-membership, parent/overlap edges, routing recall vs reference classifier.

body:
Depends on: D3 observation run (#3 in this chain). The teacher's ceiling bounds everything downstream; SmolLM2-135M is a weak teacher by design. Evaluate one larger pinned HF revision (e.g. SmolLM2-360M or Qwen-class llama-family with unsharded BF16 safetensors) through the full pipeline (download → observe → compile → cover → score → Gate C) and compare teacher floor bits/token and graph agreement against the 135M run. Acceptance: a recorded decision on the default teacher with both ceiling tables; if adopted, the pinned revision and HEAD teacher CID are updated in one commit.


---

## Definition of Done
- One larger pinned HF teacher (unsharded BF16 safetensors, llama-family) is run through the full pipeline (download → observe → compile → cover → score → Gate C).
- A default-teacher decision (stay on 135M vs adopt the larger pin) is recorded with both ceiling tables.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Teacher-floor bits/token and graph top-1 agreement reported for both teachers on the same D3 partitions.
- [ ] Artifact sizes and per-token latency included in the comparison.
- [ ] If adopted: pinned revision + HEAD teacher CID updated in one commit with the table attached.

title: D3 natural-corpus observation run (Simple English Wikipedia)

milestone.title: Phase 2: Overlapping multiresolution regions
milestone.description: Cover induction, calibrated radii, bounded multi-membership, parent/overlap edges, routing recall vs reference classifier.

body:
Depends on: nothing (can start anytime; consumes #59 pipeline). The confirmed D3 distribution includes a Simple English Wikipedia natural partition (CC BY-SA 4.0, SPDX in PROV) that does not exist as data yet. Produce it: fetch a pinned Simple Wiki snapshot, shard-generate teacher observations via the observe CLI into .uor-models, write the corpus manifest with CID + license, and record the held-out split rule. Acceptance: manifest committed under .uor-models with CID; the first full Gate C run on the natural partition prints in the issue; PROV section of produced artifacts references the corpus CID.


---

## Definition of Done
- A pinned Simple English Wikipedia corpus exists as teacher observations under `.uor-models/`, with a manifest carrying its CID and SPDX `CC-BY-SA-4.0` license.
- The held-out split rule is fixed in the manifest and the first full Gate C run on the natural partition is posted here.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Manifest committed with corpus CID, license identifier, article count, and split rule.
- [ ] Observations are generated via the observe pipeline (content-addressed sample IDs, resumable shards).
- [ ] PROV sections of artifacts built on it reference the corpus CID.
- [ ] The natural-partition Gate C 4-way table is in this thread.

title: Hidden-state trace lane for cover induction

milestone.title: Phase 2: Overlapping multiresolution regions
milestone.description: Cover induction, calibrated radii, bounded multi-membership, parent/overlap edges, routing recall vs reference classifier.

body:
Depends on: cover fineness sweep (decide whether geometry or evidence is the bottleneck first). Cover induction currently runs on the deterministic context-bundle geometry (i64 dyadic bundle). The TeacherOracle trace surface (observe.rs: hidden_state(), top_k()) exists but is unused. Wire it as a second observation lane: run cover induction over final hidden states for a fixed sample, and compare region quality (reference recall, Gate C) against the bundle lane at the same region budget. Acceptance: a per-lane comparison table and a recorded lane choice; the trace surface gains a quantized (i8) spill path per plan §4.1 memory rules if adopted.


---

## Definition of Done
- A hidden-state observation lane (TeacherOracle trace surface) exists alongside the bundle lane, and both lanes are evaluated at the same region budget.
- A lane choice (bundle / hidden-state / union) is recorded with measured evidence.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Cover induction runs end-to-end over final hidden states for a fixed observation sample.
- [ ] Per-lane reference recall and Gate C numbers are compared in one table.
- [ ] If adopted: quantized (i8) spill for hidden-state vectors per plan §4.1 memory rules, with the memory budget honored.
- [ ] If rejected: the comparison table showing no gain is attached.

title: Cover fineness sweep under the fixed scorer

milestone.title: Phase 2: Overlapping multiresolution regions
milestone.description: Cover induction, calibrated radii, bounded multi-membership, parent/overlap edges, routing recall vs reference classifier.

body:
Depends on: #64. The current induced cover is 42 regions across 3 depths — far coarser than the 4×256 TLA class grid, which is one suspected cause of residual correlation and weak emissions. Sweep region budget (42 → 128 → 512) and split entropy threshold (0.25 → 0.1 bits) and measure reference top-1/top-M recall, frontier width, artifact bytes, and Gate C top-1 agreement under the fixed formula at each point. Acceptance: a rate-distortion table (regions × bytes × agreement) and a chosen operating point with the tradeoff documented; predictive_sufficiency module reused for the depth curves where applicable.


---

## Definition of Done
- A rate-distortion sweep of the induced cover (≈42 → 128 → 512 regions; split threshold 0.25 → 0.1 bits) is measured under the fixed (#64) scorer.
- An operating point (region budget + split threshold) is chosen and recorded with the tradeoff.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Each point reports: reference top-1/top-M recall, frontier width, artifact bytes, Gate C top-1 agreement.
- [ ] Results are presented as a rate-distortion table (regions × bytes × agreement), deterministic double-run.
- [ ] The chosen point is justified against the 42-region baseline row.

title: Interaction residuals for explicit overlap nodes

milestone.title: Phase 4: Transitions and residual emission
milestone.description: Forward/reverse indexes, root priors, ScoreQ residuals, interaction residuals, bounded top-K. Gates A/C.

body:
Depends on: #64 and the cover fineness sweep. The PDF's original double-counting mitigation expects interaction residuals attached to canonical overlap nodes when overlapping memberships carry non-independent evidence. The induced cover currently has no explicit overlap nodes (only E_o edges). Evaluate whether any high-co-activation region pairs (from the E_o builder) justify materializing an overlap node with an interaction residual ΔI(pair,v) = lnP(v|pair) − lnP(v|p1) − lnP(v|p2) + lnP(v|parent), gated on held-out gain vs its bytes. Acceptance: overlap nodes exist only where the held-out bits/token improvement exceeds a documented threshold (else this issue closes as 'evaluated, not justified' with the numbers).


---

## Definition of Done
- A principled evaluation decides whether any high-co-activation region pairs justify explicit overlap nodes with interaction residuals.
- Overlap nodes exist in artifacts only where held-out gain exceeds the documented byte threshold — or the candidate is closed as evaluated-and-rejected with numbers.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Candidate pairs are selected by a documented co-activation criterion (not hand-picked).
- [ ] Per-candidate held-out bits/token delta vs artifact bytes is reported.
- [ ] If adopted: Theorem 10 non-duplication tests cover interaction entries (unique canonical contribution IDs).
- [ ] If rejected: the measured table proving non-justification is attached.

title: TRACKER: R4G1 quality-improvement chain
body:
Single map of every known way to fix and improve graph output quality, in dependency order. Root context: Gate C measured formula v1 at 0.3% vs 31.7% TLA3 baseline (2026-07-22); the deployed quality gate in src/r4g1.rs rejects graphs scoring below baseline (no-EXCT). Confirmed M.V.G. targets: docs/transformerless/BASELINE.md §4.

Chain 1 — unblock the scorer (Phase 4, now):
1. #64 chain-telescoped + EXCT precedence redesign (IN FLIGHT — everything else here depends on it)
2. F-emissions ablation decision (ΔT value test)
3. Emission smoothing calibration (add-one vs Witten-Bell vs absolute discounting)
4. Residualized EXCT for the deployed path (removes probe-time float; unblocks exact-context evidence within the integer contract)
5. Interaction residuals for explicit overlap nodes (PDF's original non-duplication mechanism; needs overlap nodes in the cover)

Chain 2 — better substrate (Phase 2/3):
6. Cover fineness sweep (region budget vs recall/bytes under the fixed formula)
7. Hidden-state trace lane for cover induction (richer representations than bundle geometry; observe.rs seam)
8. Anti-degeneracy objectives in cover scoring (J(C): invariance, reuse, MDL — Phase 3)

Chain 3 — better evidence (data):
9. D3 natural-corpus observation run (Simple English Wikipedia, CC BY-SA 4.0, CID-pinned manifest)
10. Larger/better teacher evaluation (pinned-revision swap beyond 135M; quality ceiling of the teacher bounds the graph)
11. Richer teacher evidence (top-k > 3 logits in observation records)

Chain 4 — measurement integrity:
12. HF-path (SmolLM2) full Gate C on the D3 distribution (the M.V.G. judge is currently fixture-corpus only)
13. Canonical bits/token definition (11.88 scorer accounting vs 6.54 P2 WB — one metric, documented)
14. CI Gate C trend tracking (4-way table per commit, regression alarms)

Chain 5 — deployed behavior (Phase 5):
15. ResolutionStatus-driven behavior in the deployed path (Novel/Contradictory → manifest policy: widen, EXCT, abstain)
16. Repetition control in the graph scoring path (legacy recency penalty has no graph equivalent yet)

Each issue body lists its dependencies and acceptance checks. Close this tracker when every linked issue is closed or formally descoped.


---

## Definition of Done
- Every linked issue (#66–#80) is closed — either completed with its acceptance criteria met, or formally descoped with the measured evidence recorded.
- A final quality summary is posted here: the 4-way Gate C table at chain end vs the 0.3% starting point, per the M.V.G. targets in `docs/transformerless/BASELINE.md` §4.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All 15 linked issues are closed with evidence in their threads.
- [ ] The summary table shows graph no-EXCT top-1 agreement and bits/token vs the TLA3 baseline at the end of the chain.
- [ ] Every M.V.G. target row in BASELINE.md §4 has a measured value and a plain pass/fail verdict.
